reasoning: Based on multiple converging lines of evidence from before Oct 26, 2025: (1) Carbon Brief analysis stated 2025 was 'very unlikely' to beat 2024 as the hottest year, forecasting it to finish 2nd or 3rd warmest; (2) Observed monthly anomalies in May 2025 (1.07°C) and August 2025 (1.07°C) were 0.21°C below 2024's record annual average (1.28°C); (3) La Niña conditions present in October 2025 (71% chance) following the 2024 El Niño event that boosted temperatures, providing strong cooling influence; (4) Climate scientist Zeke Hausfather predicted in January 2025 that 2025 would be among hottest years but 'likely not top the rankings'; (5) 2024's record included an unexplained ~0.2°C warming anomaly that climate scientists couldn't account for in models, making it an unusually high bar to clear. The combination of observed below-threshold monthly anomalies, cooling ENSO conditions, and expert forecasts all point to 2025 not exceeding 2024's record.
